seoTitle: Fix Shopify Product Showing Sold Out With Stock Left
metaDescription: Shopify product has inventory but still shows Sold Out? Fix location mismatches, variant issues, and sales channel settings causing the display bug.
intro: It's a common and confusing Shopify problem: your product has stock available, but the storefront still shows it as "Sold Out" or "Unavailable." This usually comes down to inventory settings, fulfillment location mismatches, unpublished sales channels, or app sync issues rather than an actual stock shortage. This guide walks through the possible causes and fixes so your inventory displays accurately to customers.

steps.heading: Check inventory settings and stock tracking
steps.body: Start by confirming that inventory tracking is enabled correctly on the product and that stock quantities are entered as expected.

steps.heading: Fix fulfillment location mismatches
steps.body: If inventory is assigned to the wrong fulfillment location, Shopify can show the product as sold out even though stock exists elsewhere, so verify the correct location is selected.

steps.heading: Review multiple product variants
steps.body: Products with several variants can show "Out of Stock" for individual options if quantities aren't set correctly across each variant, so check each one.

steps.heading: Confirm active sales channels and publishing
steps.body: A product can appear sold out if it isn't properly published to the sales channel a customer is viewing, so verify publishing status across channels.

steps.heading: Check theme and app conflicts
steps.body: Some sold-out displays are caused by theme-level logic or dropshipping apps like DSers, Zendrop, or AliExpress that need to be re-synced.

steps.heading: Save changes and verify the fix
steps.body: After adjusting settings, save and recheck the product page to confirm it now correctly shows as available.

faq.question: Why does my Shopify product show sold out when I have stock?
faq.answer: This is commonly caused by fulfillment location mismatches, incorrect variant quantities, unpublished sales channels, or app sync issues rather than an actual lack of stock.

faq.question: Can a dropshipping app cause this sold out display?
faq.answer: Yes, apps like DSers, Zendrop, or AliExpress can cause sync issues that make Shopify display a product as sold out even when inventory exists.

faq.question: Why is only one variant showing out of stock?
faq.answer: This usually happens when individual variant quantities weren't updated correctly, even if the overall product has stock.

faq.question: Does my theme affect whether sold out shows correctly?
faq.answer: Yes, some theme-level display logic can cause a sold out label to appear incorrectly, separate from your actual Shopify admin inventory settings.

faq.question: Do I need to republish the product to fix this?
faq.answer: In some cases yes — confirming the product is published to the correct active sales channels is one of the fixes for this issue.
